flashcards.back: < means "less than" (left on number line), > means "greater than" (right), = means "equal to".
Use the number line: numbers to the LEFT are smaller, numbers to the RIGHT are larger. Read left to right for least-to-greatest ordering.

flashcards.front: What happens when you multiply an inequality by a negative number?
flashcards.back: The inequality sign REVERSES. Example: 3 < 5, but 3×(-1) > 5×(-1) gives -3 > -5.
A compound inequality: x is greater than -2 AND less than 5. Graphically, x is in the interval between -2 and 5 on the number line.
